open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory.Limits
open scoped SpecOfNotation

namespace AddMonoidAlgebra
variable {M G R k H : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommGroup G] [AddCommGroup H]
  {f : (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M).LocalizationMap G} {g : (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M).LocalizationMap H}
  {s : AddSubmonoid G} {x : M √ó M}

section CommRing
variable [CommRing R] {I : Ideal R[M]}

variable (f s) in
/-- The monoid ideal corresponding to a submonoid `s` of the Grothendieck group of a monoid is an
ideal generated by binomials whose exponents differ by an element of `s`. -/
def monoidIdeal : Ideal R[M] := .span <| (fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s.subPairs f

variable (f g s) in
lemma monoidIdeal_comap :
    monoidIdeal g (s.comap (g.addEquivOfLocalizations f)) = (monoidIdeal f s : Ideal R[M]) := by
  unfold monoidIdeal
  congr!
  exact AddSubmonoid.subPairs_comap ..

/-- An ideal is toric if it's prime and a group ideal. -/
@[mk_iff isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
class IsToricIdeal (I : Ideal R[M]) extends I.IsPrime where
  /-- Use `IsToricIdeal.exists_monoidIdeal_eq'` instead. -/
  exists_monoidIdeal_eq' :
    ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup (AddLocalization (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)),
      monoidIdeal (AddLocalization.addMonoidOf (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)) s.toAddSubmonoid = I

variable (f) in
lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq :
    IsToricIdeal I ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup G, monoidIdeal f s.toAddSubmonoid = I := by
  rw [isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
  congr! 1
  refine (f.addEquivOfLocalizations <|
    AddLocalization.addMonoidOf _).comapAddSubgroup.toEquiv.exists_congr fun s ‚Ü¶ ?_
  congr! 1
  simpa using (monoidIdeal_comap ..).symm

namespace IsToricIdeal

alias ‚ü®exists_monoidIdeal_eq, of_exists_monoidIdeal_eq‚ü© := isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq

end IsToricIdeal
end CommRing

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory.Limits
open scoped SpecOfNotation

namespace AddMonoidAlgebra
variable {M G R k H : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommGroup G] [AddCommGroup H]
  {f : (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M).LocalizationMap G} {g : (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M).LocalizationMap H}
  {s : AddSubmonoid G} {x : M √ó M}

section CommRing
variable [CommRing R] {I : Ideal R[M]}

variable (f s) in
/-- The monoid ideal corresponding to a submonoid `s` of the Grothendieck group of a monoid is an
ideal generated by binomials whose exponents differ by an element of `s`. -/
def monoidIdeal : Ideal R[M] := .span <| (fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s.subPairs f

variable (f g s) in
lemma monoidIdeal_comap :
    monoidIdeal g (s.comap (g.addEquivOfLocalizations f)) = (monoidIdeal f s : Ideal R[M]) := by
  unfold monoidIdeal
  congr!
  exact AddSubmonoid.subPairs_comap ..

/-- An ideal is toric if it's prime and a group ideal. -/
@[mk_iff isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
class IsToricIdeal (I : Ideal R[M]) extends I.IsPrime where
  /-- Use `IsToricIdeal.exists_monoidIdeal_eq` instead. -/
  exists_monoidIdeal_eq' :
    ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup (AddLocalization (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)),
      monoidIdeal (AddLocalization.addMonoidOf (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)) s.toAddSubmonoid = I

variable (f) in
lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq :
    IsToricIdeal I ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup G, monoidIdeal f s.toAddSubmonoid = I := by
  rw [isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
  congr! 1
  refine (f.addEquivOfLocalizations <|
    AddLocalization.addMonoidOf _).comapAddSubgroup.toEquiv.exists_congr fun s ‚Ü¶ ?_
  congr! 1
  simpa using (monoidIdeal_comap ..).symm

namespace IsToricIdeal

alias ‚ü®exists_monoidIdeal_eq, of_exists_monoidIdeal_eq‚ü© := isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq

end IsToricIdeal
end CommRing

section

      ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : Set (M √ó M), .span ((fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I := by

    -- This involves showing s is a subgroup

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks

/-!
# Toric ideals

This file defines toric ideals.
-/

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory.Limits
open scoped SpecOfNotation

namespace AddMonoidAlgebra
variable {M G R k H : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommGroup G] [AddCommGroup H]
  {f : (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M).LocalizationMap G} {g : (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M).LocalizationMap H}
  {s : AddSubmonoid G} {x : M √ó M}

section CommRing
variable [CommRing R] {I : Ideal R[M]}

variable (f s) in
/-- The monoid ideal corresponding to a submonoid `s` of the Grothendieck group of a monoid is an
ideal generated by binomials whose exponents differ by an element of `s`. -/
def monoidIdeal : Ideal R[M] := .span <| (fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s.subPairs f

variable (f g s) in
lemma monoidIdeal_comap :
    monoidIdeal g (s.comap (g.addEquivOfLocalizations f)) = (monoidIdeal f s : Ideal R[M]) := by
  unfold monoidIdeal
  congr!
  exact AddSubmonoid.subPairs_comap ..

/-- An ideal is toric if it's prime and a group ideal. -/
@[mk_iff isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
class IsToricIdeal (I : Ideal R[M]) extends I.IsPrime where
  /-- Use `IsToricIdeal.exists_monoidIdeal_eq` instead. -/
  exists_monoidIdeal_eq' :
    ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup (AddLocalization (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)),
      monoidIdeal (AddLocalization.addMonoidOf (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)) s.toAddSubmonoid = I

variable (f) in
lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq :
    IsToricIdeal I ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup G, monoidIdeal f s.toAddSubmonoid = I := by
  rw [isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
  congr! 1
  refine (f.addEquivOfLocalizations <|
    AddLocalization.addMonoidOf _).comapAddSubgroup.toEquiv.exists_congr fun s ‚Ü¶ ?_
  congr! 1
  simpa using (monoidIdeal_comap ..).symm

namespace IsToricIdeal

alias ‚ü®exists_monoidIdeal_eq, of_exists_monoidIdeal_eq‚ü© := isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq

end IsToricIdeal
end CommRing

section
variable [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] {I : Ideal k[M]}

lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_span_single_sub_single :
    IsToricIdeal I
      ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : Set (M √ó M), .span ((fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I where
  mp := by rintro ‚ü®s, rfl‚ü©; exact ‚ü®inferInstance, _, rfl‚ü©
  mpr := by
    let G := AddLocalization (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)
    rintro ‚ü®_, s, hsI‚ü©
    letI V := Spec(k[M] ‚ß∏ I)
    let : V.Over Spec(k[M]) := sorry
    let : Spec(k[G]).Over Spec(k[M]) := sorry
    let VinterT := pullback (V ‚Üò Spec(k[M])) (Spec(k[G]) ‚Üò Spec(k[M]))
    refine ‚ü®?_‚ü©
    sorry
```

We need to prove the equivalence between `IsToricIdeal I` and `I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : Set (M √ó M), .span ((fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I`.

The forward direction `mp` is given by the definition of `IsToricIdeal`. If `I` is toric, then it's prime and there exists a submonoid `s` of `M √ó M` such that `I` is generated by binomials `x^a - x^b` for `(a, b) ‚àà s`.

For the reverse direction `mpr`, we need to show that if `I` is prime and generated by binomials `x^a - x^b` for some set `s ‚äÜ M √ó M`, then `I` is toric. This requires showing that `I` is a group ideal, which involves constructing a submonoid of the Grothendieck group.

Let's complete the proof:

/-- The monoid ideal corresponding to a submonoid `s` of the Grothendieck group of a monoid is an
ideal generated by binomials whose exponents differ by an element of `s`. -/
def monoidIdeal : Ideal R[M] := .span <| (fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s.subPairs f

variable (f g s) in
lemma monoidIdeal_comap :
    monoidIdeal g (s.comap (g.addEquivOfLocalizations f)) = (monoidIdeal f s : Ideal R[M]) := by
  unfold monoidIdeal
  congr!
  exact AddSubmonoid.subPairs_comap ..

/-- An ideal is toric if it's prime and a group ideal. -/
@[mk_iff isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
class IsToricIdeal (I : Ideal R[M]) extends I.IsPrime where
  /-- Use `IsToricIdeal.exists_monoidIdeal_eq` instead. -/
  exists_monoidIdeal_eq' :
    ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup (AddLocalization (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)),
      monoidIdeal (AddLocalization.addMonoidOf (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)) s.toAddSubmonoid = I

variable (f) in
lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq :
    IsToricIdeal I ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : AddSubgroup G, monoidIdeal f s.toAddSubmonoid = I := by
  rw [isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq']
  congr! 1
  refine (f.addEquivOfLocalizations <|
    AddLocalization.addMonoidOf _).comapAddSubgroup.toEquiv.exists_congr fun s ‚Ü¶ ?_
  congr! 1
  simpa using (monoidIdeal_comap ..).symm

namespace IsToricIdeal

alias ‚ü®exists_monoidIdeal_eq, of_exists_monoidIdeal_eq‚ü© := isToricIdeal_iff_exists_monoidIdeal_eq

end IsToricIdeal
end CommRing

section
variable [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] {I : Ideal k[M]}

lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_span_single_sub_single :
    IsToricIdeal I
      ‚Üî I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : Set (M √ó M), .span ((fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I where
  mp := by rintro ‚ü®s, rfl‚ü©; exact ‚ü®inferInstance, _, rfl‚ü©
  mpr := by
    let G := AddLocalization (‚ä§ : AddSubmonoid M)
    rintro ‚ü®_, s, hsI‚ü©
    letI V := Spec(k[M] ‚ß∏ I)
    let : V.Over (Spec { carrier := AddMonoidAlgebra k M, commRing := commRing }) := sorry
    let : (Spec { carrier := AddMonoidAlgebra k G, commRing := commRing }).Over
      (Spec { carrier := AddMonoidAlgebra k M, commRing := commRing }) := sorry
    let VinterT := pullback (V ‚Üò Spec { carrier := AddMonoidAlgebra k M, commRing := commRing })
      (Spec { carrier := AddMonoidAlgebra k G, commRing := commRing } ‚Üò
        Spec { carrier := AddMonoidAlgebra k M, commRing := commRing })
    refine ‚ü®?_‚ü©
    sorry
```

We need to prove the equivalence between `IsToricIdeal I` and `I.IsPrime ‚àß ‚àÉ s : Set (M √ó M), .span ((fun (a, b) ‚Ü¶ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I`.

The forward direction `mp` is given by the definition of `IsToricIdeal`. If `I` is toric, then it's prime and there exists a submonoid `s` of `M √ó M` such that `I` is generated by binomials `x^a - x^b` for `(a, b) ‚àà s`.

For the reverse direction `mpr`, we need to show that if `I` is prime and generated by binomials `x^a - x^b` for some set `s ‚äÜ M √ó M`, then `I` is toric. This requires showing that `I` is a group ideal, which involves constructing a submonoid of the Grothendieck group.
